skill.name: ai-music
skill.description: Generate AI music on RunComfy via the `runcomfy` CLI. Smart router across music models: ElevenLabs Music, ACE Step, ACE Step 1.5, audio-inpaint, and audio-outpaint.
skill.summary: Smart router for AI music generation across ElevenLabs, ACE Step, and ACE Step 1.5 models via the RunComfy CLI. Handles text-to-music, inpainting, and outpainting.
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: The skill is a well-documented router for the legitimate RunComfy CLI music generation service. All 72 'backtick execution' detections are false positives—they are markdown code blocks showing `runcomfy run` command examples in documentation. The 23 'hardcoded URLs' are documentation links to runcomfy.com, skills.sh, and example placeholder URLs. The 'hidden file' detection at line 259 is the documented token storage location (~/.config/runcomfy/token.json). The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections are false positives triggered by non-crypto content. The skill has transparent security practices: scope-restricted bash access (Bash(runcomfy *)), documented network allowlist, and warnings about prompt injection from third-party audio sources.

low_findings.title: Bash command scope requires user trust in runcomfy CLI
low_findings.description: The skill declares `allowed-tools: Bash(runcomfy *)`, granting permission to run any runcomfy subcommand. This is a scoped permission but still requires the user to trust the @runcomfy/cli npm package. The skill documents this clearly and provides security guidance.

low_findings.title: Token stored in filesystem by CLI
low_findings.description: The runcomfy CLI stores API tokens at ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with mode 0600. This is a standard CLI pattern but the skill documentation notes users can use RUNCOMFY_TOKEN env var to avoid file storage. Risk is minimal and transparently documented.

content.user_title: Generate AI Music with Smart Model Routing
content.value_statement: Stop guessing which AI music model to use. This skill automatically routes your request to the best RunComfy model—whether you need premium ElevenLabs vocals, cheap ACE Step drafts, multilingual songs, or audio inpainting.
actual_capabilities: Route music generation requests to the optimal model (ElevenLabs Music, ACE Step, or ACE Step 1.5) based on user intent
Generate vocal and instrumental tracks with text prompts, tags, and structured lyrics
Inpaint specific time ranges within existing audio tracks to fix bad sections
Outpaint audio tracks bidirectionally to extend intros and outros
Handle multilingual vocal generation across 50+ languages via ACE Step 1.5
Provide cost-optimized routing between premium ($0.0083/s) and budget ($0.0002/s) models
limitations: Requires a RunComfy account and API token to generate music
Audio inpainting is limited to time-bounded sections—mask-based inpainting is not available
ACE Step outpainting is capped at 4 minutes total track length
No mask-based audio editing; edits must fit the time-range schema

use_cases.title: Premium brand jingle creation
use_cases.description: Marketing teams can generate polished 5–15 second brand jingles using ElevenLabs Music for hero-quality commercial audio.

use_cases.title: Cost-effective background music library
use_cases.description: Content creators can generate 50+ background music tracks at $0.012/track using ACE Step base for podcasts, videos, or games.

use_cases.title: Multilingual song adaptation
use_cases.description: Music producers can create the same song in 8+ languages by swapping lyrics while keeping consistent style tags across ACE Step 1.5.
use_cases.target_user: Music producers and international content teams

prompt_templates.title: Generate a premium vocal track
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 60-second upbeat indie-pop anthem with female lead vocals, electric guitars, and driving drums at 120 BPM. Use ElevenLabs Music for polished quality.

prompt_templates.title: Create background music on a budget
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 90-second instrumental lo-fi hip-hop track with mellow piano, soft drums, and a chill vibe. Use ACE Step base for cost-effective generation.
prompt_templates.scenario: YouTube videos, podcasts, and content libraries

prompt_templates.title: Fix a bad section in existing audio
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a track where the second chorus sounds wrong. Use ACE Step audio-inpaint to regenerate the section from 1:20 to 1:50 with tags matching the original indie-pop style.

prompt_templates.title: Extend a short hook into a full track
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a 30-second hook I love. Use ACE Step audio-outpaint to add a 30-second intro and 60-second outro, extending it into a full 2-minute track.

output_examples.input: Generate a 30-second jingle for a coffee shop ad with warm acoustic guitar and a friendly female vocal.
output_examples.output:
Model: ElevenLabs AI Music Generation
Cost: ~$0.25
Duration: 30 seconds
Output: 44.1 kHz stereo WAV file with intro, verse, and outro sections matching the warm acoustic brief.

output_examples.input: Create 10 different background music tracks for a meditation app, each 2 minutes long.
output_examples.output:
Model: ACE Step (base)
Cost: ~$0.04 total ($0.004 per track)
Duration: 10 × 120 seconds
Output: 10 ambient tracks with varied tags (rain sounds, forest ambiance, ocean waves, etc.)

output_examples.input: My song's bridge (1:00-1:20) has clipping distortion. Fix only that section.
output_examples.output:
Model: ACE Step audio-inpaint
Input: Original track URL, tags matching original style, start_time: 60, end_time: 80
Output: Regenerated 20-second bridge section seamlessly integrated with the rest of the track.

best_practices: Draft on ACE Step base ($0.0002/s) to iterate on tags and lyrics, then finalize on ElevenLabs Music for premium quality
Use ACE Step 1.5 for multilingual vocal tracks in non-English languages (50+ language support)
Always verify the source audio URL for inpaint/outpaint operations comes from a trusted CDN to prevent indirect prompt injection
anti_patterns: Using ElevenLabs Music for high-volume batch generation—cost dominates; use ACE Step base instead
Attempting mask-based audio inpainting—not supported; use time-range inpainting or regenerate the full track
Extending tracks beyond 4 minutes via outpainting—chain multiple outpaint calls instead

faq.question: Which model should I use for commercial music?
faq.answer: Use ElevenLabs AI Music Generation for polished commercial tracks. It offers 44.1 kHz stereo, section-level control, and premium vocal quality at $0.0083/second.

faq.question: How much does it cost to generate a 1-minute track?
faq.answer: ACE Step base: ~$0.012. ACE Step 1.5: ~$0.018. ElevenLabs Music: ~$0.50. ACE Step is about 27× cheaper than ElevenLabs.

faq.question: Can I generate music in languages other than English?
faq.answer: Yes. ACE Step 1.5 supports 50+ languages for vocal generation. Use the same tags and swap the lyrics to your target language.

faq.question: How do I fix a bad section in an existing track?
faq.answer: Use ACE Step audio-inpaint. Provide the source audio URL, specify start_time and end_time in seconds, and include tags matching the original style.

faq.question: Is there a limit on track length for outpainting?
faq.answer: Yes. ACE Step audio-outpaint is capped at 4 minutes total track length. For longer extensions, chain multiple outpaint calls.

faq.question: Do I need a RunComfy account?
faq.answer: Yes. Run `runcomfy login` after installing the CLI, or set the RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable in CI/container environments.
